trigger.reason: TechCrunch reports on agentic AI swarms ('loopy') advancing beyond single-agent systems — a capability leap likely to trigger sympathy-fade selling in automation-adjacent software, RPA, BPM, and low-code platforms.

primary_event.headline: The AI world is getting 'loopy'
primary_event.summary: TechCrunch reports on a new agentic AI capability enabling swarms of agents to work continuously and autonomously in the background. This represents a step forward in agentic AI maturity, moving from isolated agent tasks to coordinated, persistent multi-agent systems.

secondary_events.headline: Google DeepMind bets $75M on AI's future in Hollywood with A24 deal

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed the 'AI world is getting loopy' TechCrunch piece on agentic AI swarms, with hypothesized blast radius in RPA/BPM/low-code/workflow automation.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger targets automation/workflow software, but the candidate basket is dominated by semi-cap names (up on the day, not victims) and two software names (PHR down, DOCN down). PHR's healthcare intake business is moated by HIPAA/EHR integration plumbing — not a workflow-automation target. DOCN is an AI-inference beneficiary mislabeled as commodity cloud.

discoveries.name: Phreesia, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors mention AI as a general competitive concern ('developments in AI may reduce demand for our solutions' and AI lowering market-entry costs for competitors), but the core moat is described as deep EHR/PM integrations, HIPAA compliance, payer eligibility, and patient-facing workflows tied to healthcare-services-client contracts — not generic workflow automation an agent swarm can replicate.
discoveries.thesis: Agentic swarms target generic enterprise workflow automation; Phreesia's intake/engagement business is gated by HIPAA, EHR/PM integration plumbing, payer-specific eligibility, and life-sciences network contracts. A 'loopy' agent swarm cannot drop into a Cerner/Epic integration or take over patient-payment processing. The sympathy drop on a sub-average-volume day looks thematic, not fundamental.
discoveries.what_confirms: Bounce in the next 3-5 sessions without a follow-up healthcare-specific AI announcement; stable analyst estimates; no client-loss commentary.
discoveries.what_kills: An EHR vendor (Epic/Cerner) announcing native agentic intake; a Phreesia customer-cohort or net-retention deterioration in next print; a new risk-factor amendment naming agentic AI specifically.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Healthcare-workflow SaaS sells off on every generalist agentic AI headline, but HIPAA + EHR integration moats make these names structural false-victims. Track the pattern.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Patient intake workflows are exactly the kind of 'repetitive enterprise workflow' retail assumes agent swarms will eat, despite the regulated integration layer underneath.
research_pointers: Check most recent quarterly net revenue retention and DBA client count trend
Verify no large EHR partner has announced competing native intake agents
Confirm AccessOne payment-processing volume trend is stable (separate revenue driver, unaffected by agentic AI)

discoveries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K explicitly positions AI/ML as a growth vector ('A growing part of our business involves AI/ML products and services, and we are devoting significant resources to develop and deploy such strategies') — DOCN is selling inference compute to agentic workloads, not being replaced by them. No risk-factor language identifies agentic AI as a substitution threat.
discoveries.thesis: Agentic AI swarms that 'run continuously in the background' need somewhere to run — and DOCN's developer-cloud + GPU/inference offering is positioned as a host for exactly these workloads. Selling DOCN on an agent-swarm headline confuses the picks-and-shovels case with the workflow-software case. The drop on near-average volume is pure thematic SaaS-cloud sympathy.
discoveries.what_confirms: Mean-reversion bounce; positive commentary on AI/ML revenue mix in next print; no SMB churn signal.
discoveries.what_kills: A hyperscaler launching a developer-tier inference product priced below DOCN; a guide-down on AI/ML revenue; SMB customer-count deterioration.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Commodity-cloud names get sold on every AI capability headline despite being net beneficiaries of inference demand — the framing flip from 'cloud victim' to 'inference host' is the trade.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Agent swarms running continuously = continuous inference compute demand; DOCN is a target host venue for developer-built agents, not a target victim.
research_pointers: Check AI/ML revenue disclosure trend in most recent quarter
Verify GPU droplet / Paperspace inference product traction commentary
Look for any hyperscaler pricing announcement on the same day that could be the real catalyst

skipped.reason: Up on the day — semi-cap process control is not in the agentic-AI blast radius and the price reaction confirms it; nothing to fade.
Up on the day — power-delivery for fabs/data centers is an AI-capex beneficiary, not a sympathy victim of an agentic-AI headline.

_trigger.headline: The AI world is getting 'loopy'
_trigger.summary: TechCrunch reports on a new agentic AI capability enabling swarms of agents to work continuously and autonomously in the background. This represents a step forward in agentic AI maturity, moving from isolated agent tasks to coordinated, persistent multi-agent systems.
